sections.paragraph:
Γαββαθᾶ indecl. (an Aram. word whose mng. is still uncertain. Acc. to Jos., Bell. 5, 51 Γαβὰθ Σαούλ is to be rendered λόφος Σαούλου) Gabbatha, a locality in Jerusalem which also had the Gk. name Λιθόστρωτον (q.v.) J 19:13. CTorrey ZAW 65, ’53, 232f thinks the word is Latin, viz. gabata=‘platter’ and was adapted to Aramaic; the stone pavement resembled such a dish.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Gk. Gk. = Greek

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

ZAW ZAW = Zeitschrift für die Alttestamentliche Wissenschaft—Lists 6

viz. viz. = videlicet (namely)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 185
